title: paspalum laeve var. australe
intro: Paspalum laeve var. australe, also known as Dallol or Blue Grama, is a type of grass native to Australia and is considered a medicinal plant. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been shown to aid in wound healing and reduce pain. The health benefits of Paspalum laeve var. australe include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and provide relief from digestive problems. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, and ointments, and are typically used to treat a range of health issues, from minor skin irritations to more serious conditions such as bronchitis.
